metadata.label: Provenance
metadata.value: Sel.2.81 inscribed on title page 'Jo: Blemel'. Former library classmark (crossed through) L.1.33. From the Royal Library, the collection of Bishop John Moore given to the Library in 1715 by George I.

metadata.label: Abstract
metadata.value: Fuch’s herbal is regarded as perhaps the most beautiful of all printed herbals; with over 500 woodcuts, many of the illustrations are based on plants grown in Fuchs’s own garden. The 54 surviving hand-coloured copies of the first edition, as here, were coloured according to his own specific guidelines, and based on a set of original drawings now lost. This copy also bears annotations by an early English owner, adding the vernacular names for many plants.

metadata.label: Title
metadata.value: De historia stirpium commentarii insignes: maximis impensis et uigiliis elaborati, adiectis earundem uiuis plusquam quingentis imaginibus, nunquam antea ad naturæ imitationem artificiosius effictis & expressis

metadata.label: Note(s)
metadata.value: 509 full page woodcuts (included in pagination); portraits of author (title page verso) and illustrators (penultimate leaf recto) Illustrated by Heinricus Füllmaurer, Albertus Meyer ("pictores") and Vitus Rodolph. Speckle ("sculptor") Names of plants in Latin, Greek and German.

metadata.label: Donor(s)
metadata.value: George, I, King of Great Britain, 1660-1727

metadata.label: Subject(s)
metadata.value: Botany -- Pictorial works -- Early works to 1800; Botany -- Pre-Linnean works; Botany, Medical -- Early works to 1800
